title: Programming and DS - Data Structures - Binary Search Tree

questions.question:
You are given the postorder traversal, \( P \), of a binary search tree on the \( n \) elements \( 1,2,\dots,n. \) You have to determine the unique binary search tree that has \( P \) as its postorder traversal. What is the time complexity of the most efficient algorithm for doing this?



Data Structures: NIELIT 2016 MAR Scientist C
options: None of the above, as the tree cannot be uniquely determined.

questions.question:
____ is the worst-case time complexity for all operations (i.e.,) search, update and delete) in a general Binary Search tree



Data Structures: NIELIT 2018
options: \( O( \log n) \) for search and insert, and \( O(n) \) for delete

questions.question:
When we perform in order traversal on a binary tree, we get the ascending order array. The tree is:



Data Structures: NIELIT Scientific Assistant A 2020 November: 93
